text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is to set SMART goals: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. For example, instead of "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% in three months." To stay on track, break the project into milestones or smaller steps, such as completing initial research or testing features. Each milestone will produce a deliverable, like a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, such as team members or clients. This way, everyone understands the objectives and works together effectively.
